title: Intracranial Hemorrhage and Stroke in the Neonate
authors: Cizmeci MN, de Vries LS / Fanaroff & Martin 2025

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Intracranial Hemorrhage and Stroke?
บทนี้ครอบคลุม 2 major brain injury ที่ส่งผลต่อชีวิตทารกในระยะยาว: (1) GMH-IVH ใน preterm — ยังคง incidence ~25% ใน VLBW; severe forms (grade 3 + PVHI) → CP, cognitive impairment, shunt; (2) Perinatal stroke — PAIS 20–30/100,000 คน common cause ของ neonatal seizures; CSVT → thalamic hemorrhage, epilepsy. PHVD เป็น treatable complication ที่ต้องวินิจฉัยก่อน symptoms — cUS criteria-based early intervention ลด VPS rate เหลือ 19% และ improve NDI (ELVIS trial 2019). UCM เพิ่ม severe GMH-IVH ใน infants <27 wk vs DCC (8% vs 3% JAMA 2019) — ข้อมูลที่เปลี่ยน practice ไปทั่วโลก. DRIFT therapy — first intervention to show sustained 10-year cognitive benefit. เรารู้จักทุก pathway แล้ว — แต่ต้อง act early, measure correctly, intervene before symptoms.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย incidence ของ GMH-IVH ตาม gestational age (Lai meta-analysis 2022; VON 2020 database) และ timeline ที่ hemorrhage เกิดขึ้น
บรรยาย neuropathology ของ GMH-IVH: germinal matrix anatomy, blood supply (Heubner artery), venous drainage, mechanism ของ PVHI vs IVH extension
จำแนก grading ของ GMH-IVH: Papile (CT, 1978) vs Volpe (cUS) vs Canadian Consensus (Mohammad 2021) — และ clinical relevance ของแต่ละ grade
ระบุ risk factors ของ GMH-IVH: prenatal (chorioamnionitis, antenatal steroids), intrapartum (mode of delivery, DCC vs UCM), postnatal (respiratory disturbances, hemodynamic, genetic)
อธิบาย PHVD: pathogenesis (3 mechanisms), measurements (VI, AHW, TOD), risk zones (green/yellow/red), AAP 2020 cUS screening schedule
อธิบาย management ของ PHVD: supportive care → LP → temporizing neurosurgical interventions → VPS; DRIFT trial results; ELVIS trial (LT vs HT thresholds)
บรรยาย neurodevelopmental outcomes ตาม grade: low-grade GMH-IVH (grades 1–2), high-grade (grade 3 + PVHI), cerebellar hemorrhage (CHOPIN study)
อธิบาย prevention ของ GMH-IVH: antenatal steroids (RR 0.58 Cochrane 2020), DCC vs UCM, nursing bundles (de Bijl-Marcus 2020), QI interventions (Kramer 2022)
วินิจฉัย CSVT: risk factors (term IVH + unilateral thalamic hemorrhage), MR venography, anticoagulation controversy (25% US vs 69% international)
วินิจฉัย PAIS: incidence 20–30/100,000, focal clonic seizures, MCA involvement (left > right 3–4x), DWI ใน acute phase, Wallerian degeneration, outcomes
ระบุ future directions: MSC therapy (Ahn 2018 phase I), CSF biomarkers (IL-6, S100, TGF-β, GFAP) สำหรับ early detection
1. GMH-IVH — Incidence และ Neuropathology
1.1 Incidence
ยุค CT (1970s): incidence ~50% ใน BW <1500 g; ลดลงหลังจาก cUS + improved neonatal care
Lai et al meta-analysis 2022 (37 studies, 87,993 infants): any-grade GMH-IVH: ~45% ที่ GA <25 wk; ~34% ที่ GA <28 wk; ~17% ที่ GA 28–31 wk
VON database 2020 (47,324 VLBW infants): severe GMH-IVH (grade 3 or 4) = 7.8%; sevenfold higher risk in <27 wk vs 27–32 wk (50% vs 7%)
Timing: >90% เกิดภายใน 3 วันแรก; ~50% เกิดภายใน 6 ชั่วโมงแรก; ~67% ภายใน 24h; lesion reaches maximal extent 3–5 วันหลัง initial detection; <10% เกิดหลัง 1 สัปดาห์
Outborn infants: 2× higher rate vs inborn (large US database: 27.4% vs 13.4%; adjusted OR 1.75); off-peak delivery (12am–7am): OR 1.39 สำหรับ severe GMH-IVH
1.2 Germinal Matrix Anatomy
Germinal matrix = highly cellular/vascular region ระหว่าง subependymal region + lateral ventricle; present GW 23–32; involute completely โดย GW 34–36
Blood supply: primarily Heubner artery (branch of anterior cerebral artery); also anterior choroidal artery + terminal branches of lateral striate arteries
Venous drainage: fan-shaped medullary veins → terminal vein (below germinal matrix) → internal cerebral vein; this venous anatomy creates vulnerability to venous stasis → PVHI
Vessels = 'immature vascular rete' (primitive endothelial lining, paucity of tight junction proteins, no pericytes) → susceptible to rupture
Site: most abundant over caudate head/body; less mature infants → body; more mature → head; ~50% co-existing choroid plexus hemorrhage; temporal + occipital sites detected only on MRI/SWI
1.3 Types of Intracranial Hemorrhage

rows: Subependymal; cyst follows after weeks; detected by cUS
<50% ventricular area; no ventricular distension; Volpe: 10–50%
Large IVH with acute ventricular distension
>50% ventricular area; AHW >6 mm; often → PHVD
Ipsilateral periventricular; fan-shaped; 90% unilateral; mortality 40%; causes hemiplegia
cUS via mastoid window 9% incidence; MRI up to 19–37%
Tear of dura / bridging vein rupture
Birth trauma; mostly term; asymptomatic or seizures/lethargy

blocks.text:
IVH Grading — Papile vs Volpe vs Canadian Consensus (Mohammad 2021)
Papile 1978 (original CT-based): I = subependymal; II = IVH without dilation; III = IVH + dilation; IV = IVH + parenchymal hemorrhage (later recognized as PVHI not extension). Volpe (cUS): grade 1 = GMH <10% ventricular area; grade 2 = IVH 10–50%; grade 3 = IVH >50% + acute dilation; PVHI = separate notation. Canadian Consensus (Mohammad J Front Pediatr 2021): grade 1 = GMH region only, no blood in ventricle; grade 2 = blood in ventricle <50%, no distension; grade 3 = blood in ventricle + AHW >6 mm (acute distension); PVHI = separate notation (fan-shaped, ipsilateral, venous infarction mechanism). Clinical note: PVHI is NOT 'grade 4' — it is a separate entity with distinct mechanism (venous stasis → infarction), unilateral 90%, ipsilateral grade 3 IVH in >80%.
2. Pathogenesis และ Risk Factors
2.1 Pathogenesis — 3 Categories
Vascular factors: immature capillary integrity (simple endothelial lining, paucity tight junctions, pericyte deficiency); variant subependymal venous anatomy → narrower terminal vein curvature

Intravascular factors: (a) ↑ cerebral venous pressure: prolonged labor, dystocia, RDS, head positioning; (b) ↓ blood flow: systemic hypotension; (c) fluctuating CBF: hypercarbia, rapid volume expansion, hypoglycemia, rapid Hb drop
Extravascular factors: deficient extracellular + vascular matrix support (pericytes), tight junction proteins
2.2 Prenatal Risk Factors

Antenatal corticosteroids: strongest preventive agent — McGoldrick et al Cochrane 2020 (12 studies, 8475 infants): RR 0.58; 95% CI 0.45–0.75 — moderate-certainty evidence; mechanism: direct brain maturation + ↓ RDS + ↓ inotrope requirement

Histologic chorioamnionitis: NOT associated with GMH-IVH (Bierstone JAMA Pediatr 2018); Clinical chorioamnionitis: OR 1.62 (1.17–2.24) for severe IVH (Canadian Neonatal Network multicenter) — important distinction
Pre-eclampsia → REDUCED risk of GMH-IVH (enhanced in utero brain maturation)
Fetal growth restriction: not associated with ↑ brain injury on cUS but ↑ mortality
2.3 Intrapartum Risk Factors

Mode of delivery (German Neonatal Network, 2203 VLBW 220/7–366/7 wk): planned CS 17.2% vs vaginal 26.6% vs emergency CS 31.1%; vaginal OR 1.7 (1.3–2.2); emergency CS OR 1.9 (1.3–2.7); แต่ในกลุ่ม 20,231 infants <34 wk → CS not associated after confounding adjustment (adjusted OR 0.99)
Delivery room resuscitation: 8685 infants <30 wk: those receiving DR resuscitation → OR 1.47 (1.23–1.74) for grades 3–4 GMH-IVH

DCC vs UCM vs ICC — Network Meta-Analysis (Jasani et al JAMA Pediatr 2021)
56 studies, 6852 preterm infants. Key findings: DCC vs ICC: OR 0.73 (0.54–0.97) for GMH-IVH. UCM vs ICC: OR 0.58 (0.38–0.84) for GMH-IVH. CRITICAL: Katheria et al JAMA 2019 (RCT, prematurely terminated) — UCM vs DCC ใน 23–27 wk: severe GMH-IVH 22% vs 6% (risk difference 16%; 95% CI 6–26%; P=.002). Practice implication: UCM should NOT be used ใน infants <28 wk GA — DCC (60 sec) เป็น preferred strategy. Note: APLOTS RCT (Tarnow-Mordi NEJM 2017) — DCC vs ICC (10 sec) in <30 wk: no difference in composite outcome (37% vs 37.2%; RR 1.00).
2.4 Postnatal Risk Factors
Volume-targeted ventilation (VTV): Klingenberg et al Cochrane 2017 (22 studies, 1065 infants): VTV → RR 0.53 (0.37–0.77) for grades 3–4 GMH-IVH; NNT=11
High tidal volumes >6 mL/kg ใน DR PPV → ↑ GMH-IVH (prospective observational); LISA surfactant during CPAP vs intubation → 10.3% vs 22.1% severe GMH-IVH (RCT, 211 infants 23–27 wk)

Cerebral autoregulation impairment: pressure-passive circulation → cerebral hyperperfusion → ↑ GMH-IVH; NIRS monitoring: ↓ cerebral oxygenation + impaired autoregulation = early warning; Early NIRS multicenter study (Chock et al J Pediatr 2020): adverse outcomes ↔ ↓ mean cerebral oxygenation
COL4A1/COL4A2 mutations: atypical PVHI presentation → test for collagen mutations; factor V Leiden + prothrombin G20210A: limited evidence in preterm (MTHFR 1298A>C possibly associated)
3. Diagnosis — cUS Protocol และ PHVD Monitoring
3.1 Clinical Presentations (3 Syndromes)
Catastrophic deterioration: sudden ↑ O2 requirement, hemodynamic deterioration, drop in BP, acidosis; ↓ hematocrit without clear change in condition
Saltatory syndrome: gradual onset; change in spontaneous general movements
Silent/asymptomatic (most common): detected only by routine cUS screening; no obvious clinical signs
3.2 cUS Technique and Windows
Primary window: anterior fontanelle (coronal + parasagittal views)
Posterior fontanelle: ↑ detection ของ small IVHs ใน posterior horn; mastoid window: detects CBH >4 mm; temporal window: temporal/occipital GMH sites
MRI advantage: more sensitive for temporal + occipital GMH, subtle WMI, small CBH; SWI sequence detects CBH in 37% vs 9–19% by cUS; cUS remains first-line: bedside, no sedation, repeatable
3.3 AAP 2020 + Inder 2021 cUS Screening Protocol

rows: Every other week until 34 wk PMA
MRI at term (if significant risk factors)

blocks.text: Rationale for more frequent scanning: 95% ของ IVH เกิดภายใน postnatal day 7; serial scans detect PHVD ก่อน clinical symptoms หลายสัปดาห์; enable early intervention
3.4 PHVD Monitoring — Ventricular Measurements

Ventricular Index (VI): longest horizontal distance midline → lateral border of ventricle (coronal, plane of 3rd ventricle); Levene & Starte reference curves GA 27–40 wk; Brouwer et al Radiology 2012: new reference values GA 24.7–42.6 wk (625 neonates)
Anterior Horn Width (AHW): longest oblique distance just anterior to thalamic notch; >3 mm = ventriculomegaly; >6 mm = 2 SD above normal; >10 mm = high-risk zone
Thalamo-Occipital Distance (TOD): occipital horn depth sagittal plane; >25 mm = elevated risk; FOHR/FTHR: strong correlation with ventricular volumes

PHVD Risk Zones (El-Dib et al J Pediatr 2020)
Green zone: VI ≤97th percentile AND AHW ≤6 mm → observe closely, cUS twice weekly until stable ×2 weeks, then every 1–2 wk until 34 wk PMA. Yellow zone: VI >97th percentile AND AHW >6 mm AND/OR TOD >25 mm → refer to regional center for neurosurgical review; consider LP 2–3 times; cUS 2–3 times weekly. Red zone: VI >97th percentile +4 mm AND AHW >10 mm AND/OR TOD >25 mm — OR any clinical signs (HC >2 cm/wk, separated sutures, bulging fontanelle) → consider LP 2–3 times + neurosurgical intervention (temporizing or VPS). Key principle: Waiting for clinical signs = late intervention = preventable brain injury.
4. PHVD Management — From LP to VPS
4.1 Initial Supportive Care
Minimal handling; prevent BP fluctuations + CO2 swings; prevent ventilator asynchrony; optimize coagulopathy; blood transfusion ถ้า large GMH-IVH + significant Hb drop
aEEG monitoring: detect electrographic-only seizures (no phenobarb pre-emptively); fibrinolytic therapy: disappointing + risk of rebleeding → NOT recommended
4.2 Lumbar Puncture (LP) for PHVD
LP ไม่ได้ prevent PHVD ใน meta-analysis; แต่เป็น first-step treatment เมื่อ criteria met
Target: remove 10 mL/kg CSF; cUS before + after LP to assess effect; up to 3 LPs before escalating to neurosurgical intervention
Mechanism benefit: physical removal of CSF containing blood products + protein → ↓ neuroinflammatory/neurotoxic reactions + ↓ extracellular matrix deposition + re-establish normal CSF drainage
4.3 DRIFT Trial — Drainage, Irrigation, Fibrinolytic Therapy
Phase I (Whitelaw Pediatrics 2003): reduced need for VPS to 26% vs ~60% historical; promising pilot

RCT (Whitelaw Pediatrics 2007, n=70, GA 24–34 wk): DRIFT vs standard (reservoir taps); composite death+VPS not significantly different (44% vs 50%); DRIFT group: 35% secondary IVH vs 8% standard (P significant) → ↑ blood transfusions
2-year follow-up (Whitelaw Pediatrics 2010): DRIFT survivors: 31% severe cognitive disability vs 59% standard; adjusted OR 0.17 (0.05–0.57)

10-year follow-up (Luyt et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020): mean cognitive score 69.3 (DRIFT) vs 53.7 (standard); adjusted P=.01; survival without severe cognitive disability 66% vs 35% (P=.003) — first intervention for PHVD showing sustained cognitive improvement at 10 years
4.4 ELVIS Trial — Early vs Late Intervention Thresholds

ELVIS Trial (de Vries LS et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2019; n=126, GA <34 wk)
LT (Low-Threshold) group: intervene when VI approaching 97th+4mm line but not yet crossed; AHW >6mm approaching 10mm; TOD >25mm. HT (High-Threshold) group: intervene when VI >97th+4mm AND AHW >10mm. Both groups asymptomatic at time of intervention. Primary outcome: VPS rate: LT 19% vs HT 23% (lowest reported in literature; no significant difference). MRI substudy (Cizmeci J Pediatr 2019): LT group had smaller ventricular volumes, lower FOHR, lower global brain abnormality scores, more WM+GM volume, less delayed myelination + corpus callosum thinning. 2-year outcome (Cizmeci J Pediatr 2020): adjusted OR 0.24 (0.07–0.87) for composite adverse outcome in LT group (post-hoc, adjusted for GA + GMH-IVH severity + CBH). Conclusion: earlier intervention based on cUS measurements may prevent further brain injury.
4.5 Temporizing Neurosurgical Interventions

Options (per American Academy of Neurological Surgeons): ventricular reservoir (VAD) — most commonly used; ventriculosubgaleal shunt — typically when weight >700 g; both have similar complication rates

VAD meta-analysis (Lai et al J Pediatr 2021, 59 studies): VPS conversion rate 60.5%; moderate-severe NDI 34.8%; infection 8.2%; revision 14.6%; death 12.9%; older age at temporizing procedure → ↑ VPS conversion + ↑ NDI (P<.001)
Neuroendoscopic lavage (NEL): washes hemorrhagic CSF + removes clots; more effective than intermittent VAD taps; requires considerable expertise
Intraventricular urokinase (Park et al Childs Nerv Syst 2021): 9 infants; 6000 IU/mL; 14 days; VPS rate 14%; no secondary hemorrhages; promising but preliminary
5. Outcomes ของ GMH-IVH
5.1 Mortality
Christian et al J Neurosurg Pediatr 2016 (147,823 infants): mortality by grade: grade 1: 4%; grade 2: 10%; grade 3: 18%; PVHI: 40%
Netherlands + Canada (Cizmeci J Pediatr 2020): PVHI mortality 40%; redirection of care rate 78%
5.2 Low-Grade GMH-IVH (Grades 1–2): NDI Risk
NICHD RCT (Payne et al JAMA Pediatr 2013, 1472 infants <27 wk): low-grade GMH-IVH NOT associated with adverse NDI vs no hemorrhage in adjusted analysis

Contradicting data (Bolisetty et al Pediatrics 2014): low-grade GMH-IVH → ↑ neurosensory impairment (22% vs 12.1%), CP (10.4% vs 6.5%) vs no IVH; OR 2.24 (1.21–4.16) for CP (Hollebrandse Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2021 at age 8 yr)
Long-term follow-up (Ann Wy et al J Perinatol 2015): no difference in cognitive/academic/behavioral outcomes at 18 yr — controversy unresolved
5.3 High-Grade GMH-IVH and PVHI
CP rates by grade (Law et al J Pediatr 2021): grade 1: 6%; grade 2: 16%; grade 3: 33%; PVHI: 64%
PVHI outcomes (Bassan et al Pediatrics 2007): abnormal muscle tone 60%; visual field defects 26%; gross motor delay 73%; fine motor delay 59%; expressive language 38%; cognitive impairment 50%
EPIPAGE (n=1812, <33 wk, 5yr follow-up): CP rates: grade 1 8%; grade 2 11%; grade 3 19%; PVHI 50%

PVHI location predicts deficit: posterior frontal/parietal → motor (hemiplegia); anterior frontal + temporal → cognitive, visual, behavioral; GMFCS I/II in 81% of PVHI survivors with CP (Cizmeci J Pediatr 2020)
Predicting hemiplegia early: asymmetric PLIC myelination on T1 at term (absent/asymmetric high signal) + corticospinal tract visualization on DTI — high sensitivity at 4 weeks postnatal
VPS presence as predictor: MDI <70 in 43% grade 3 no-VPS vs 60% with VPS; 48% PVHI no-VPS vs 76% with VPS; timing of intervention appears more important than VPS presence itself
5.4 Cerebellar Hemorrhage (CBH) Outcomes
CHOPIN study (Boswinkel et al Cerebellum 2019, 177/218 infants, 2yr follow-up): NDI in punctate CBH 40%; limited CBH 51%; massive CBH 78%; punctate vs limited: no significant difference (P=.42)

Isolated CBH (Limperopoulos Pediatrics 2007, n=35): neurologic abnormalities in 2/3 vs 5% controls; severe motor disability 48%; delayed expressive language 42%; cognitive deficits 40%; autism screening 37%; vermis involvement → worse outcomes
Systematic review (Hortensius et al Pediatrics 2018): cognitive 38%, motor 39%, language 41%, behavioral 38% delay in isolated CBH; punctate CBH = better prognosis; large CBH = worst
Volumetric MRI CBH at 4.5 yr: ↑ CBH size + vermis location → ↓ preschool motor + visuomotor function + ↑ externalizing behavior, independent of supratentorial injury
6. Prevention ของ GMH-IVH
6.1 Proven / Evidence-Based
Antenatal steroids: McGoldrick Cochrane 2020: RR 0.58 (0.45–0.75), moderate-certainty — single most effective intervention

Antenatal MgSO4: Doyle et al Obstet Gynecol 2009: RR 0.69 (0.54–0.87) for CP; Rouse NEJM 2008 RCT: ↓ moderate/severe CP (1.9% vs 3.5%; RR 0.55); no significant reduction in large GMH-IVH (2.1% vs 3.2%)

Prophylactic indomethacin: Cochrane 2010 (19 trials, 2872 infants): ↓ high-grade GMH-IVH (pooled RR 0.66 95% CI 0.53–0.82); no effect at 8 yr (Vohr Pediatrics 2003); gender effect: indomethacin → halved IVH in males + eliminated parenchymal hemorrhage + higher verbal scores 3–8 yr (Ment J Pediatr 2004)

Nursing care bundles (de Bijl-Marcus Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020): head midline + ↑ incubator head end + avoid rapid blood withdrawal + avoid sudden leg elevation → ↓ GMH-IVH in Dutch tertiary centers

QI project (Kramer et al Pediatrics 2022): targeted interventions (head midline, early NCPAP, rescue antenatal steroids, risk-based indomethacin): severe GMH-IVH 14% → 1.2%; mortality ↓ 50%; sustained over 2.5 years
6.2 Limited/Controversial

Antenatal phenobarbital: Crowther et al Cochrane 2010: significant reduction in meta-analysis of all trials (RR 0.65); effect disappeared with only high-quality trials included (RR 0.90) → not recommended routinely
Antenatal vitamin K: nonsignificant reduction after excluding quasi-randomized trials → not recommended
Prophylactic ibuprofen: Ohlsson Cochrane 2020: possible decrease in high-grade GMH-IVH (RR 0.67; moderate-quality); larger trials needed
7. Perinatal Stroke — CSVT และ PAIS
7.1 Cerebral Sinovenous Thrombosis (CSVT)
Incidence: 41/100,000 newborns (Canadian Pediatric Ischemic Stroke Registry); higher in Dutch population-based study; 43% ของ all childhood CSVT = neonates
Timing: 2/3 present within 48h of birth; symptoms can develop 10–14 days postnatal (associated with dehydration or infection)
Risk Factors for CSVT
Perinatal asphyxia 24%; skull molding during delivery; dehydration; infection; maternal fever/chorioamnionitis; HI injury; thrombophilia
ECMO: 29% of cases; congenital heart disease 23%; CHD surgery: CSVT in 28% post-neonatal cardiac surgery
IVH in term infant with unilateral thalamic hemorrhage → ALWAYS suspect CSVT (Wu et al Ann Neurol 2003: 4/5 with IVH + thalamic hemorrhage had CSVT vs 5/21 IVH only)
Most common sinuses: superior sagittal + transverse (Canadian registry); straight sinus most common + thalamic hemorrhage (Dutch study — Berfelo Stroke 2010)
Diagnosis and Management
Seizures: most common presenting symptom (70%); also lethargy, full fontanelle, prominent scalp veins
Best imaging: MR venography (absence of flow in affected sinus); color Doppler cUS: absent/decreased flow — practical screening tool; contrast CT no longer recommended (empty delta sign)
Anticoagulation controversy: US centers treat 25% vs other countries 69%; no RCT; recent meta-analysis (Bhatt & Chan Fac Rev 2021): anticoagulation → ↓ thrombus propagation + no ↑ morbidity/mortality
Outcomes of CSVT
>90% survival; 48–61% moderate-severe disability; 9% recurrence
Fitzgerald et al Arch Neurol 2006 (42 neonates): 60% parenchymal infarcts; 79% any impairment; 67% CP; 59% cognitive impairment; 41% epilepsy
Thalamic hemorrhage → electrical status epilepticus during sleep (ESES) in early childhood → adverse cognitive outcomes
7.2 Perinatal Arterial Ischemic Stroke (PAIS)

Incidence: 20–30/100,000 live births (much higher than previously estimated); 2nd most common cause of neonatal seizures; preterm incidence 32/100,000 (German population-based, Sorg et al Neonatology 2021) — higher than term
Left > right hemisphere 3–4×: hemodynamic differences at birth + PDA + preferential left common carotid flow; MCA involved 2× more than any other artery
Risk Factors for PAIS
Maternal: infertility, pre-eclampsia, prolonged ROM, chorioamnionitis, hypoglycemia (independent risk factors in case-matched study)
Infant: male gender (OR 2.8), family history seizures (OR 6.5), ≥1 intrapartum complication (OR 5.8), ≥2 complications (OR 21.8)
Special associations: cardiac disease, ECMO, transposition of great arteries (stroke in 31%, especially after balloon atrial septostomy); portal vein thrombosis; cocaine
Preterm PAIS risk factors DIFFER from term: twin-twin transfusion syndrome (OR 31.2), fetal heart rate abnormality (OR 5.2), hypoglycemia (OR 3.9) — from Benders et al Stroke 2007
Diagnosis
Focal clonic seizures: most common presentation (72% in Kirton et al IPSS Pediatrics 2011, n=248); subtle in preterm; may present late as hand preference in infancy

DWI (diffusion-weighted MRI) — gold standard: cytotoxic edema → high signal acutely; also shows acute corticospinal tract involvement at midbrain → predicts Wallerian degeneration + hemiplegia (Kirton Stroke 2007)
cUS: unreliable in first days; by end of first week: wedge-shaped echogenicity in arterial territory with linear demarcation line; cystic evolution 4–6 weeks
MR angiography: can show dissection/occlusion; may miss small vessel occlusion; EEG: focal electrographic seizures + ipsilateral background suppression → highly suggestive of PAIS
Wallerian degeneration: mesencephalon asymmetry visible on MRI 4–6 weeks after onset → predict hemiplegia
Management and Outcomes
Treatment: mainly supportive (treat hypoglycemia, seizures → antiepileptic medication; most discontinue before discharge)
No thrombolytic therapy for neonatal PAIS (outside 6-hour hypothermia window); safety study with intranasal MSCs (Baak et al Lancet Neurol 2022, 10 infants)

Wagenaar et al Pediatrics 2018 (neurodevelopmental outcomes): almost half (46%) doing well at median 41 months; CP ~32%; epilepsy 11–67% depending on follow-up length; language impaired 49%; post-neonatal epilepsy mean onset 4 yr
CP predominantly spastic hemiplegia (upper limb > lower limb; GMFCS I/II majority; most walk before 18 months)

Brain reorganization: Staudt et al Ann Neurol 2004: contralesional hemisphere develops ipsilateral corticospinal pathways → mediate useful (not normal) hand function; ipsilesional reorganization = less effective
8. NICU Practical — IVH Prevention and PHVD Management
8.1 Prevention Protocol (Delivery Room + NICU)
ห้องคลอด: DCC 60 วินาทีใน <30 wk; ห้ามใช้ UCM ใน <28 wk (เพิ่ม severe GMH-IVH 22% vs 6%); gentile DR PPV — tidal volume ≤6 mL/kg; skim surfactant via LISA ถ้าทำได้
24 ชั่วโมงแรก: head midline position เสมอ; ↑ incubator head end 15–30 degrees; หลีกเลี่ยง rapid bolus IV; หลีกเลี่ยง ยก leg ขณะ care; minimal handling

Hemodynamic: optimize BP (avoid pressure-passive cerebral circulation); ป้องกัน PaCO2 swings (ventilator synchrony); ป้องกัน hypoglycemia; ตรวจ hemoglobin (anemia → ↑ CBF fluctuation); พิจารณา prophylactic indomethacin ในผู้ชาย ELBW (ตาม unit protocol)
Antenatal: steroids ทุก preterm <34 wk (แม้จะ <6 ชั่วโมงก่อนคลอด — rescue course); MgSO4 ใน <32 wk; deliver ใน tertiary center (avoid outborn)
8.2 cUS Screening เพื่อ Detect PHVD Early
ทำ cUS ตาม protocol (<28 wk: day 1/3/7/14/21/28 จากนั้น every 2 wk ถึง 34 wk PMA); plot VI + AHW + TOD ทุกครั้ง
ถ้า GMH-IVH ใด grade ≥2: เพิ่มความถี่ cUS เพื่อจับ PHVD ก่อน clinical symptoms
Action ตาม risk zone: green = observe closely; yellow = LP 2–3 ครั้ง + neurosurgical consult; red = LP + neurosurgical intervention (VAD/subgaleal shunt) + MRI at term
บันทึก VI + AHW + TOD ทุก cUS บน risk zone chart — ตัวเลขที่ trend เป็น rising มีค่ากว่า single cutoff
8.3 Term Neonate with IVH — Suspect CSVT
Always ask: term baby + IVH ± unilateral thalamic hemorrhage = CSVT until proven otherwise
Order: MR venography (หรือ color Doppler cUS ถ้า MRI ไม่พร้อม); ตรวจหา risk factors (dehydration, infection, thrombophilia)
Discuss anticoagulation ร่วมกับ pediatric neurology/hematology — ข้อมูลสนับสนุนการใช้ หากไม่มี contraindication
8.4 Neonatal Focal Seizures — Suspect PAIS
Focal clonic seizures ใน term/near-term infant ที่ไม่มี clear HIE = PAIS until proven otherwise
Order MRI (DWI sequence) เร็วที่สุด — DWI positive ตั้งแต่ acute phase; treat seizures + ตรวจหา underlying causes
ไม่ต้อง extensive thrombophilia workup routinely (genetic prothrombotic disorder unlikely to be causative)
9. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — UCM ห้ามใช้ใน <28 wk: เพิ่ม Severe IVH ถึง 22%
Katheria et al JAMA 2019 (RCT, prematurely terminated): umbilical cord milking (UCM) vs delayed cord clamping (DCC) ใน 23–27 wk infants — severe GMH-IVH 22% (UCM) vs 6% (DCC); risk difference 16%; P=.002. Jasani JAMA Pediatr 2021 network meta-analysis: DCC ≈ UCM overall, แต่ DCC ดีกว่าชัดเจนใน <28 wk. Practice: DCC 60 วินาที คือ standard ใน <30 wk; UCM contraindicated ใน <28 wk; ใน >30 wk อาจยืดหยุ่นได้มากกว่า.

PEARL 2 — PHVD: Intervene Before Symptoms, Not After
Clinical signs ของ PHVD (bulging fontanelle, separated sutures, head circumference >2 cm/wk, sunsetting eyes) = LATE findings — ปรากฏหลัง cUS evidence หลายสัปดาห์. ELVIS trial: VPS rate เหลือ 19–23% เมื่อ intervene based on cUS measurements while asymptomatic (ลดลงจาก 60% historical). Leijser Neurology 2018: early intervention → outcomes comparable with no-intervention group; late intervention → significantly worse. Rule: ถ้ามี GMH-IVH grade 3 หรือ PVHI → plot VI/AHW/TOD ทุก cUS และ act เมื่อ yellow zone (อย่ารอ red zone).

PEARL 3 — Term Infant IVH + Unilateral Thalamic Hemorrhage = CSVT
Full-term neonate ที่มี intraventricular hemorrhage (ไม่ใช่ preterm IVH) ร่วมกับ unilateral thalamic hemorrhage — CSVT คือ diagnosis ที่ต้องนึกถึงเสมอ. Wu et al Ann Neurol 2003: 4 ใน 5 infants ที่มี IVH + thalamic hemorrhage → confirmed CSVT; เทียบกับ 5 ใน 21 ที่มี IVH อย่างเดียว. Straight sinus thrombosis → thalamic hemorrhage; superior sagittal/transverse sinus → parenchymal infarct. Diagnosis: MR venography. ≥70% present with seizures. พิจารณา anticoagulation ร่วมกับ expert (ข้อมูลล่าสุดสนับสนุนการ prevent propagation).

PEARL 4 — PVHI ≠ Grade IV IVH: เป็น Venous Infarction คนละ Mechanism
Papile 1978 เรียก PVHI ว่า 'grade 4 IVH' และคิดว่าเป็น extension ของ IVH เข้า parenchyma — แต่ผิด. Ependymal lining intact ใน PVHI; mechanism คือ periventricular venous stasis → congestion → ischemia → hemorrhagic infarction. PVHI: unilateral 90%; ipsilateral grade 3 IVH ใน >80%; fan-shaped; parietal lobe most common. Location predicts deficit: posterior frontal/parietal → hemiplegia; anterior frontal/temporal → cognitive + behavioral. Asymmetric PLIC myelination at term → predict hemiplegia early.

PEARL 5 — DRIFT: First PHVD Treatment with 10-Year Cognitive Benefit
DRIFT (Drainage, Irrigation, Fibrinolytic Therapy) มี secondary IVH ↑ vs standard (35% vs 8%) ใน RCT 2007 — แต่ follow-up ระยะยาวพิสูจน์ว่า cognitive benefit จริง. 10-year follow-up (Luyt et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020): mean cognitive score 69.3 (DRIFT) vs 53.7 (standard); survival without severe cognitive disability 66% vs 35% (adjusted P=.003). DRIFT = first + only PHVD intervention with proven sustained long-term cognitive improvement. Phase II MSC trial (NCT02890953) กำลัง analyze — future direction promising สำหรับ PHVD treatment.
10. Evidence Update — Key Studies

Lai GY et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2022;107:513: meta-analysis 87,993 infants: GA-based GMH-IVH pooled estimates; any-grade IVH 45% <25 wk, 34% <28 wk, 17% 28–31 wk — establishes GA-specific baseline incidence
VON Database 2020 (47,324 VLBW infants): severe GMH-IVH 7.8%; sevenfold risk difference <27 wk vs 27–32 wk; most comprehensive current benchmark
Inder TE et al J Pediatr 2021;237:276: neuroimaging of preterm brain — recommended cUS schedule; key finding: >90% GMH-IVH within first 3 days; rationale for frequent serial cUS in <28 wk
Katheria A et al JAMA 2019;322:1877: UCM vs DCC RCT: severe GMH-IVH 22% vs 6% in 23–27 wk; risk difference 16% — trial-changing result that removed UCM from <28 wk practice
Jasani B et al JAMA Pediatr 2021;175:e210102: network meta-analysis 56 studies 6852 infants; DCC OR 0.73 vs ICC; UCM OR 0.58 vs ICC; DCC = UCM overall; but UCM warning for <28 wk
McGoldrick E et al Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2020;12:CD004454: antenatal corticosteroids: RR 0.58 (0.45–0.75) for GMH-IVH (12 studies, 8475 infants); moderate-certainty evidence

Karimy JK et al Nat Med 2017;23:997: TLR4/NF-κB-dependent inflammatory response in choroid plexus → 3× ↑ CSF secretion after GMH-IVH in rat model — novel mechanism of CSF hypersecretion contributing to PHVD pathogenesis
Mohammad K et al Front Pediatr 2021;9:618236: Canadian consensus GMH-IVH grading: grade 1–3 + PVHI notation; standardized PVHI definition; aimed at cross-disciplinary homogeneity

El-Dib M et al J Pediatr 2020;226:16: PHVD management clinical tool: risk zones (green/yellow/red) based on Brouwer reference curves; integrate VI + AHW + TOD; evidence-based intervention decision framework

de Vries LS et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2019;104:F70 (ELVIS trial): LT vs HT intervention thresholds in progressive PHVD; VPS rate 19% vs 23% (lowest in literature); asymptomatic intervention criterion
Cizmeci MN et al J Pediatr 2020;226:28 (ELVIS 2-year outcomes): LT group: adjusted OR 0.24 (0.07–0.87) for composite adverse outcome — supports early cUS-based intervention

Luyt K et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020;105:466 (DRIFT 10-year): mean cognitive score 69.3 vs 53.7 (DRIFT vs standard); survival without severe cognitive disability 66% vs 35% (adjusted P=.003) — sustained benefit at 10 years
de Bijl-Marcus K et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020;105:419: nursing care bundles (head midline, ↑ incubator, avoid rapid procedures) → ↓ IVH incidence in Dutch multicenter cohort
Kramer KP et al Pediatrics 2022;149:e2021050652: QI project: severe GMH-IVH rate 14% → 1.2% (sustained ×2.5 yr); mortality ↓ 50% — benchmark for bundle-based prevention

Dunbar M et al Pediatrics 2020;146:e2020013201: population-based birth prevalence PAIS; corrected for underdiagnosis; 20–30/100,000 full-term; preterm incidence higher — establishes true modern incidence
Wagenaar N et al Pediatrics 2018;142:e20174164: PAIS neurodevelopmental outcomes: 46% doing well; CP 32%; language impairment 49%; epilepsy developing over time — comprehensive outcome profile

Ahn SY et al Stem Cells Transl Med 2018;7:847: phase I dose-escalation MSC trial PHVD (9 preterm infants): no adverse effects; confirms safety/tolerability → Phase II (NCT02890953) now complete, under analysis
Berfelo FJ et al Stroke 2010;41:1382: Dutch neonatal CSVT cohort: straight sinus most common; thalamic hemorrhage common; seizures in 70%; MR venography = gold standard for diagnosis
Wu YW et al Ann Neurol 2003;54:123: IVH in term neonates caused by sinovenous thrombosis — 4/5 term IVH + thalamic hemorrhage confirmed CSVT; landmark link establishing rule for clinical practice
11. Self-Check Q&A
Q: GMH-IVH incidence ตาม gestational age และ timing ที่ hemorrhage เกิด — cite ข้อมูลเฉพาะ

ตอบ: Lai meta-analysis 2022: any-grade IVH: 45% <25 wk; 34% <28 wk; 17% 28–31 wk. VON 2020: severe GMH-IVH 7.8%; sevenfold ↑ risk <27 wk. Timing: >90% ใน 3 วันแรก; ~50% ใน 6 ชั่วโมงแรก; ~67% ใน 24h; 95% ภายใน postnatal day 7; maximal extent ที่ 3–5 วัน. Outborn: OR 1.75 สำหรับ GMH-IVH; off-peak delivery (12am–7am): OR 1.39 สำหรับ severe GMH-IVH.
Q: อธิบาย mechanism ของ PVHI — ทำไมจึงไม่ใช่ 'extension of IVH'?

ตอบ: PVHI mechanism: periventricular venous stasis → ↑ capillary pressure → congestion → ischemia → hemorrhagic infarction (ไม่ใช่ blood seeping through ependyma). Ventricular ependymal lining intact ใน most PVHI. Evidence: Takashima 1986; Dudink 2008 — subtypes relate to affected veins; complete terminal vein infarction → all developed hemiplegia. Clinical features: unilateral 90%; ipsilateral grade 3 IVH >80%; parietal lobe most common; PVHI หนุ่มๆ เห็นเป็น fan-shaped/triangular → evolve to cyst in weeks; ≠ porencephalic cyst of AIS.
Q: UCM vs DCC: ข้อมูล key study ที่เปลี่ยน practice และ current recommendation

ตอบ: Katheria JAMA 2019 (RCT stopped early): UCM vs DCC ใน 23–27 wk — severe GMH-IVH 22% vs 6%; risk difference 16% (6–26%; P=.002). Jasani JAMA Pediatr 2021 (network meta-analysis): overall UCM = DCC; but subgroup <28 wk → UCM significantly ↑ GMH-IVH. Current recommendation: DCC 60 วินาทีเป็น standard ใน <30 wk; UCM is CONTRAINDICATED ใน <28 wk. APLOTS RCT (Tarnow-Mordi NEJM 2017): DCC 60 vs ICC 10 sec → no difference in composite outcome (37% vs 37.2%) — DCC ลด IVH แต่ overall composite similar.
Q: PHVD risk zones: measurements ที่ต้องวัด และ action ที่ต้องทำในแต่ละ zone

ตอบ: Measurements: VI (ventricular index, coronal), AHW (anterior horn width, oblique at thalamic notch), TOD (thalamo-occipital distance, sagittal). Green zone: VI ≤97th + AHW ≤6 mm → cUS twice weekly. Yellow zone: VI >97th + AHW >6 mm and/or TOD >25 mm → neurosurgical consult + LP 2–3×. Red zone: VI >97th+4 mm + AHW >10 mm and/or TOD >25 mm — หรือมี clinical signs → LP + neurosurgical intervention (VAD/subgaleal shunt ± VPS). TEA-MRI ทุก risk group.
Q: ELVIS trial: threshold หลัก 2 กลุ่ม, VPS rate, และ implication

ตอบ: LT (low-threshold): VI approaching 97th+4mm line (not yet crossed) + AHW >6mm approaching 10mm + TOD >25mm. HT (high-threshold): VI >97th+4mm + AHW >10mm. Both groups: asymptomatic at time of intervention. VPS rate: LT 19% vs HT 23% — lowest reported in literature. MRI substudy: LT smaller ventricles, less brain abnormality, more WM+GM volume, less myelination delay. 2-year outcome: LT adjusted OR 0.24 (0.07–0.87) for adverse composite. Bottom line: intervene early, based on measurements, while asymptomatic → protect the brain.
Q: Term infant + focal clonic seizures: วินิจฉัย PAIS อย่างไร และ management หลัก

ตอบ: Clinical clue: term/near-term infant, focal clonic seizures, ไม่มี HIE history → PAIS until proven otherwise. Imaging: MRI ± DWI ทันที — DWI shows cytotoxic edema ตั้งแต่ acute phase; left hemisphere + MCA territory most common; corticospinal tract DWI → predict hemiplegia. Management: supportive; treat hypoglycemia; antiseizure medication (most can stop before discharge); no routine thrombophilia workup (recurrence rate very low). Wallerian degeneration visible on MRI 4–6 wk → asymmetric PLIC myelination at term → confirm hemiplegia risk.
Q: CSVT: สัญญาณที่ต้องระวังในทารกครบกำหนด และ treatment controversy

ตอบ: Red flags: term neonate + IVH ± unilateral thalamic hemorrhage = CSVT until proven otherwise (Wu Ann Neurol 2003: 4/5 infants confirmed). Seizures 70%; full fontanelle; prominent scalp veins. Best imaging: MR venography (absent flow in sinus); color Doppler cUS practical screening. Treatment controversy: US 25% treat vs international 69%; no RCT; recent meta-analysis: anticoagulation → ↓ thrombus propagation + no ↑ morbidity/mortality → most experts now lean toward treating non-bleeding CSVT. ~25% show propagation if untreated; straight sinus thrombosis → thalamic hemorrhage → ESES in childhood.
Q: DRIFT trial: benefits, risks, และ 10-year outcome ที่ทำให้ therapy นี้ unique

ตอบ: Concern: RCT 2007 (n=70): secondary IVH 35% (DRIFT) vs 8% (standard); no significant difference in primary outcome (death+VPS). 2-year follow-up (Whitelaw Pediatrics 2010): severe cognitive disability 31% vs 59% (adjusted OR 0.17). 10-year follow-up (Luyt Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020): mean cognitive score 69.3 vs 53.7; survival without severe cognitive disability 66% vs 35% (adjusted P=.003). Why unique: DRIFT = first + only PHVD intervention to show sustained 10-year cognitive improvement in RCT. Secondary IVH ↑ แต่ long-term cognitive outcome ดีกว่าอย่างมีนัยสำคัญ.
Q: Nursing bundle สำหรับ prevention of GMH-IVH: interventions ใดบ้าง และ evidence

ตอบ: de Bijl-Marcus et al Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2020 (Dutch multicenter): bundle: (1) head midline position; (2) ↑ head of incubator; (3) หลีกเลี่ยง rapid blood flushing/withdrawal; (4) ไม่ยก leg ขณะ infant care → ↓ IVH incidence. Kramer et al Pediatrics 2022 (single-center QI): เพิ่ม: (5) early NCPAP; (6) rescue antenatal betamethasone; (7) risk-based indomethacin → severe GMH-IVH 14% → 1.2%; mortality ↓ 50%; sustained ×2.5 years. Rationale: ทุก intervention target hemodynamic stability + ↓ cerebral venous pressure + ↓ CBF fluctuations ใน pressure-passive preterm circulation.
Q: CBH classification, incidence, และ NDI rates ตาม size

ตอบ: Classification: punctate (<4mm, MRI only); limited (>4mm <1/3 hemisphere); large (>1/3 hemisphere). Incidence: cUS mastoid window 9%; MRI 19%; 3T-MRI SWI 37% ใน VLBW. CHOPIN study NDI at 2 yr: punctate 40%; limited 51%; massive 78% — punctate vs limited: no significant difference (P=.42). Limperopoulos 2007: isolated CBH → severe motor disability 48%; cognitive deficits 40%; autism screening 37% vs 0% controls. Vermis involvement = worst prognosis. Note: small punctate CBH on MRI = generally better preschool outcome.

Day 153 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.56
บทต่อไป: Chapter 56 — Hypoxic-Ischemic Encephalopathy (HIE). เตรียมตัว: pathophysiology of HI injury — primary + secondary energy failure; excitotoxicity + apoptosis cascade; Thompson score, Sarnat staging, amplitude-integrated EEG (aEEG) — when to cool; therapeutic hypothermia protocol: eligibility criteria, 33.5°C ×72 hours, rewarming; adjunct therapies: EPO, melatonin, xenon, stem cells — current evidence; MRI at 5–7 days: patterns of injury (watershed vs deep nuclear), DWI to predict NDI; long-term outcomes — Bayley scores, CP rates, epilepsy.
